summary: Token refresh retry can loop indefinitely when the refresh endpoint returns 401, and the new branch is untested.

issues.title: Infinite retry loop on persistent 401 from refresh endpoint
issues.evidence: retryRefresh() calls itself on any failure (line 42) without decrementing or bounding attempts; a permanent 401 (revoked refresh token) never terminates, so the request hangs and spins.
issues.suggested_fix: Bound retries (e.g. max 2) and break out on 401/403 by surfacing a re-login error instead of retrying.

issues.title: No test for the refresh-failure path
issues.evidence: Tests cover the happy-path refresh only; the new failure/retry branch (refresh.ts:38-50) has no test, so the loop bug above could regress silently.
issues.suggested_fix: Add a test where the refresh endpoint returns 401 and assert it stops after the retry cap and throws a re-login error.
